summary: Validated the 7 new features of iter8: (1) MeasurementModel.ordem present + measurements sort by ordem asc; (2) 12+ vibracao/ambos defects, 6+ termografia/ambos, 'Equipamento em Bom Estado' seeded with alarme=OK/tipo=ambos; (3) /api/audit/deletions returns 200 for admin and 403 for viewer, and delete diagnostic/measurement generate audit log entries; (4) /vibracao card ESF-0001 / Motor 01 shows 'Pico: 9.2 mm/s @ 1HV' + timeline dots; (5) machine-modal lists 3 points (1HV=9.2, 1HA=0.85, 2HV=2), modal-trend-{id} opens trend-modal with 3 data points (3.1 → 5.8 → 9.2 for 1HV); (6) Tabela de Dados filtered by ESF-0001/Motor 01 shows 3 rows × 3 date columns (15/04, 15/05, 15/06 2026); (7) /diagnostico defects filtered by machine.tipo — for a vibracao machine, only vibracao/ambos defects (12 items) render, 'Equipamento em Bom Estado' included, 'Ponto Quente em Conexão Elétrica' excluded; (8) del-diag-hist-{id} button visible in expanded machine modal in history view for admin; (9) /auditoria audit-table renders for admin (3+ rows); (10) nav-auditoria hidden for viewer, and direct /auditoria access as viewer yields 0 rows (backend 403); (11) BibliotecaDefeitos cards show Vibração/Termografia/Ambos badges + defect-field-tipo select has 3 options.
passed_backend: GET /api/measurements: all items sorted asc by 'ordem'; ESF-0001/Motor 01 returns 9 items sorted by ordem (0,0,0,1,1,1,2,2,2) — 1HV/1HA/2HV

GET /api/defects: 12 defects with tipo in [vibracao,ambos], 6 with tipo in [termografia,ambos], 'Equipamento em Bom Estado' present with alarme=OK/tipo=ambos, 'Ponto Quente em Conexão Elétrica' has tipo=termografia
POST /api/diagnostics then DELETE /api/diagnostics/{id} appends 1 log with entity_type='diagnóstico', user_name='Administrador', user_email='admin@fssolucoes.com', description contains machine_tag
POST /api/measurements then DELETE /api/measurements/{id} appends 1 log with entity_type='medição', description contains 'TEST_PT'
passed_frontend: /vibracao admin: card 'ESF-0001 / Motor 01' → peak 'Pico: 9.2 mm/s @ 1HV', timeline has 6 status dots + last diag date 02/07/2026
machine-modal: 3 rows — 1HV=9.2 mm/s RMS, 1HA=0.85 g Pico, 2HV=2 mm/s RMS; modal-trend-{id} present on each row
trend-modal for 1HV: title 'Tendência — 1HV (RMS)', 3 recharts dots + 3 x-axis ticks (3 dates)
tab-tabela + filter ESF-0001/Motor 01: 3 rows (1HV/1HA/2HV) + 3 date columns [15/04/2026, 15/05/2026, 15/06/2026]; values match 3.1/5.8/9.2 for 1HV

/diagnostico admin (cards): defects list for ESF-0001/Motor 01 (vibracao) shows 12 items including 'Equipamento em Bom Estado'; 'Ponto Quente em Conexão Elétrica' and other termografia-only defects hidden
/diagnostico history view: clicking history-row opens modal with del-diag-hist-{id} buttons for admin (multiple diags present)
/defeitos: cards show Vibração/Termografia/Ambos badges (all 3 types); new-defect modal has defect-field-tipo select with options ['vibracao','termografia','ambos']
Viewer RBAC: nav-auditoria hidden; /auditoria direct visit returns 0 rows (backend 403 logged in console)

minor.issue: Legacy measurements (imported before iter8, e.g. BBA-0001/Motor rows) don't have the 'ordem' field in their DB documents; API returns them as-is without backfilling. MeasurementModel default=0 only applies to POST validation, not GET responses. This breaks strict clients expecting 'ordem' on every row, though the frontend tolerates missing values via `m.ordem || 0`. Consider a one-time migration `$set ordem: 0 where ordem is missing` or backfill in the endpoint response.

issues: Duplicate defect 'Bom estado' (categoria=mecanico, tipo=vibracao, alarme=OK) exists alongside seed 'Equipamento em Bom Estado' (categoria=geral, tipo=ambos). Appears to be test data debris. Cleanup recommended so users see only the canonical seed.

React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' — the visual-editor tooling injects <span> inside <option>. Not a functional bug; warning-only in dev; will be a no-op in production build.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py line ~597: GET /api/measurements sorts by [('ordem',1),('data',1)] which is correct, but legacy documents without 'ordem' will sort as null/absent (Mongo sorts BSON null first ascending) — this is why the mixed dataset test failed. Add response-side backfill: `for it in items: it.setdefault('ordem', 0)` before returning.

server.py DEFAULT_DEFECTS: 'Aquecimento Excessivo (Termografia)' has categoria='termografia' but no explicit 'tipo' key, relying on seed_data's fallback (categoria=='termografia' → tipo='termografia'). This is fine but implicit — recommend adding tipo explicitly to every seed for readability.

Auditoria.js: uses `formatDateTime` from lib/dates but no error/loading state on the API call — silent noop on 403 (viewer). Fine for the RBAC design but a small toast/info banner ('Acesso restrito a administradores') would improve UX.

Diagnostico.js line 242: filter `machine.tipo === 'ambos' || d.tipo === 'ambos' || d.tipo === machine.tipo` — correct logic, tested passing. For machines with tipo=='ambos', all defects are shown, which matches PRD.

Vibracao.js peakByMachine: still unit-blind (compares valor across mm/s and g). For ESF-0001/Motor 01 the peak is unambiguously 1HV=9.2 mm/s > 2HV=2.0 mm/s > 1HA=0.85 g, but a machine where g values are numerically larger than mm/s could produce a misleading 'peak' — same comment as iter7.
action_items: OPTIONAL: Backfill missing 'ordem' field on legacy measurement documents (e.g., migration `db.measurements.update_many({ordem: {$exists: false}}, {$set: {ordem: 0}})`) or default in the GET response.
OPTIONAL: Delete stray 'Bom estado' defect duplicate from prior test iterations to keep the Biblioteca clean.

success_rate.backend: 91% (10/11 assertions — 1 minor issue on legacy 'ordem' backfill)

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted 1 TEST_ diagnostic + 1 TEST_ measurement via API to exercise the audit log — 2 audit entries remain in deletion_logs (expected, since the log is the artifact under test). The 9 seed measurements + 1 seed A2 diagnostic on ESF-0001/Motor 01 were preserved.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter8 covered ordem sort, audit log RBAC + create/delete flow, machine-modal + trend-modal, tabela de dados pivot, defect filter by machine.tipo, del-diag-hist button, /auditoria admin, viewer RBAC nav + 403. Regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter8_ordem_audit_defects.py. Data-testid with slashes/spaces needs JS querySelector with double quotes (e.g. document.querySelector('[data-testid="vibracao-card-ESF-0001 / Motor 01"]')). Note: Chromium's innerText respects CSS text-transform:uppercase; use textContent when comparing against DOM text like Biblioteca badges ('Vibração' → displayed as 'VIBRAÇÃO' in innerText but 'Vibração' in textContent).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no critical bugs. The only backend finding is that pre-existing measurements lack the newly introduced 'ordem' field. Root cause: MeasurementModel.ordem default=0 only fills the field on validation of new POST bodies; the seed migration for measurements did not backfill legacy docs, and the GET endpoint returns raw find() docs without applying the Pydantic model. Mitigation: run one-time `db.measurements.update_many({ordem:{$exists:false}}, {$set:{ordem:0}})` OR add `it.setdefault('ordem', 0)` in list_measurements() before returning.
